Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LUKE GARVEY, Esq. of Colla & Ray LLP (Tel: 415-579-1414) for plaintiffs;
Atty. P. MARK MAHONEY, Esq. of BHC Law Group LLP (Tel: 510-658-3600) for defendant Mid-Century Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipuation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. Matter discussed as to deadline for amended responses.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Interrogatories: Granted as to interrogatories 2, 3, 4, 5 and 7; denied as to form interrogatory 50.2. The motion is timely as plaintiffs filed an amended response. To require a defendant to file a motion on the matters not addressed in the amended response would not promote judicial economy and appears contrary to the language of CCP Sec. 2030.300(e) which refers to "any" supplemental verified response. (See Rutter, Civil Pro. Before Trial, Sec. 8:1146; Golf & Tennis Pro Shop v. Sup. Ct. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 127.) Contention interrogatories are well recognized and these are contention interrogatories. (CCP Sec. 2030.010(b).) The answer to interrogatory 50.2 appears complete particularly given the specific allegations of the complaint. No sanctions awarded.

Plaintiffs to serve amended responses by March 17, 2023.

Counsel for defendant Mid-Century Insurance to prepare and submit a proposed order to plaintiff's counsel for approval as to form, and then submit the proposed order to the pro tem judge at rkane1089@aol.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
